name: Eastern Asturian-Leonese

ancestors.notes: Leonese trunk; eastern varieties are more heavily Castilianised.

notes: Eastern Asturian-Leonese. Grounded in Morala & Egido (2009) and the Propuesta de Norma Ortográfica. EASTERN-SPECIFIC FEATURES (diverging from western standard): (1) NO ei/ou decreasing diphthongs — eastern varieties have already monophthongised: *queso* (not *queisu*), *poco* (not *poucu*) (Source A p. 8 — explicit contrast with western; CONFIDENCE: HIGH). (2) FINAL -o (not -u): eastern varieties preserve Latin -O as -o rather than raising to -u (Source B §2.4; Source A p. 8; CONFIDENCE: HIGH). (3) -KT- > /tʃ/ more likely in eastern areas: *fechu, muchu, derechu* rather than western *teitu, muito* (Source B §3.3; CONFIDENCE: MEDIUM — less fully attested for a purely eastern norm). (4) INCIPIENT YEÍSMO: /ʎ/ shows variation with [ʝ], under Castilian pressure (Cano González 2009). SHARED WITH ALL LEONESE: ⟨x⟩=/ʃ/, ⟨ll⟩=/ʎ/ as default, ⟨ch⟩=/tʃ/ from PL-/CL-/FL-, ⟨y⟩=/ʝ/ from -LJ-, ⟨ñ⟩=/ɲ/ — inherited from parent spec.

sources.author: Morala Rodríguez, J.R. & Egido Fernández, M.C.
sources.title: A vueltas con una norma para el leonés
sources.publisher: Instituto Castellano y Leonés de la Lengua, Colección Beltenebros 23
sources.notes: ISBN: 978-84-936383-8-2. Confirms eastern Leonese lacks ei/ou diphthongs and is more Castilianised (p. 8).

sources.author: Anonymous
sources.title: Propuesta de Norma Ortográfica para el Leonés
sources.notes: Notes -o preservation and -KT->ch as legitimate for non-western zones. Year is approximate (undated document).

sources.author: García Arias, X.Ll.
sources.title: Gramática histórica de la lengua asturiana
